title: Equivalence Relations and Partitions
description: A visual explanation showing how an equivalence relation on a set partitions it into disjoint equivalence classes
elements: Elements a, b, c (Members of Set S)
relationships: The equivalence relation ~ defines a rule for grouping elements
Each equivalence class contains all elements related to each other
The partition P is formed by the collection of all distinct equivalence classes
Every element of S belongs to exactly one equivalence class in P
The equivalence classes are disjoint and their union equals the original set S
